title: keepwright project configuration
description: Declarative configuration produced by detection + the setup wizard and consumed by the deterministic apply engine.

project.description: Human-readable project name. Fills the {{PROJECT}} placeholder.

repoOwner.description: Repository owner/org. Derived from repo when omitted.

maintainer.description: Maintainer name used for authorship and CODEOWNERS.

language.description: Output language for GENERATED artifacts (CLAUDE.md prose, rule docs, messages). Read from ~/.claude settings 'language'; the plugin itself stays in English. English when absent.

mode.description: setup = greenfield/first install; audit = report integration coverage only; maintain = re-apply deltas + derived rules on an existing repo.

stack.description: Detected primary stack, e.g. nextjs-serverless, nextjs, node-cli, python-fastapi, deno, go, rust, monorepo.

layers.description: Pipeline layers derived from the real structure, e.g. [routes, actions, lib, db, integrations].

deploy.description: Deploy variant chosen by stack; selects the deploy workflow template.

runner.description: GitHub Actions runner target. self-hosted keeps CI minutes at zero.

auth.description: Claude auth for the AI review/mention workflows. oauth = subscription token via /install-github-app (no metered cost); apikey = ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (pay per use).

criticalFiles.description: Glob/paths flagged as critical; the heuristic review warns when they change.

customValidators.description: Names of project-specific validators to scaffold into scripts/validators/.

derivedPatterns.description: Patterns derived from the repo by the derive-patterns workflow; feed the generated validators and the AI review.

design.description: Design/architecture conventions found in the repo (naming, layering, error handling, boundaries).

voice.description: Writing-voice conventions found in the repo (commit style, UI copy tone, doc register, banned terms).

issues.description: Automatic issue triage. The triage workflow classifies new issues via GitHub Models (free in Actions) and a deterministic job applies only advisory labels — never closes, assigns, or merges.

triage.description: github-models runs the classifier free over the GITHUB_TOKEN; off makes the triage workflow a no-op.

model.description: GitHub Models model id for the classify step, e.g. openai/gpt-4o-mini.
